sensitivity_note: Large capacity increases delay before spilling but does not remove object-lifetime logic.
Higher HBM bandwidth weakens movement-delay pressure but not capacity/provenance pressure.
Low fabric bandwidth makes branch/state movement more expensive; high bandwidth makes placement more flexible.
Higher CPU DRAM bandwidth strengthens warm-state spill tiers.
Low host link capability penalizes CPU/NVMe spill and migration.

calibration_source: semantic cache evidence and deferred constants
